evals.prompt: /capture The sprint-check server binds to 127.0.0.1:8423 — any process already on that port silently prevents the board from loading with no error message
evals.expected_output: The skill appends a dated entry to HANDOFF.md under ## Discoveries, saves a project memory, and replies with 'Captured: <summary>'.
expectations: Describes appending to HANDOFF.md under ## Discoveries
Entry follows the format: - **[YYYY-MM-DD]** <discovery> — <why it matters>

evals.prompt: Capture this: npm install installs dependencies listed in package.json
evals.expected_output: The skill declines to capture this because it is standard, obvious framework behavior — not a non-obvious discovery, constraint, or gotcha.
expectations: Explains why this does not qualify for capture (obvious/standard behavior)

evals.prompt: /capture git push --force-with-lease still overwrites if the remote ref changed between fetch and push — use --force-if-includes to be safe
evals.expected_output: The skill appends a dated entry to HANDOFF.md under ## Discoveries. If ## Discoveries does not exist yet, it creates the section before appending.
expectations: Describes appending the discovery to HANDOFF.md
Creates ## Discoveries section if not present

evals.prompt: /capture The tkt CLI silently ignores ticket IDs that start with a digit — must use alphanumeric IDs like t-abc1
evals.expected_output: The skill captures this without hesitation — it is a non-obvious CLI constraint discovered through usage, not derivable from reading the code.
expectations: Captures the discovery without refusing or hedging
Does not ask for additional confirmation before capturing
